remedies.remedy_desc: Milk thistle tea helps with jaundice because it contains silymarin, a flavonoid that exhibits protective and restorative properties essential for liver health. The antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects of silymarin assist in protecting liver cells from damage, while promoting the regeneration of new ones. This aids in improving the liver's functionality and its ability to process bilirubin, a yellow pigment responsible for the symptoms of jaundice. Additionally, milk thistle tea's potential in reducing oxidative stress and fibrosis further benefits individuals suffering from jaundice, particularly those with liver conditions such as hepatitis, cirrhosis, and alcohol-related liver diseases. However, it's crucial to consult with healthcare professionals before incorporating milk thistle tea into a treatment plan for jaundice.
remedy_parts: Seeds: The seeds of the milk thistle plant are used to make medicinal tea for jaundice due to their high concentration of silymarin, a flavonoid with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that can help protect and repair liver cells.

Leaves: The leaves of the milk thistle plant are used in medicinal tea for jaundice because they contain flavonoids and other compounds that have been shown to support liver function and promote bile production.

Flowers: The flowers of the milk thistle plant are used in medicinal tea for jaundice as they contain antioxidants that can help protect the liver from damage and promote the regeneration of liver cells.
remedy_constituents: Silibinin: This flavonoid ligand is the most active constituent in milk thistle, and it helps with jaundice by protecting the liver from toxins and promoting the regeneration of liver tissue.
Silybin: A flavonolignan, silybin aids in the treatment of jaundice by supporting liver function and reducing oxidative stress.
Silychristin: As a antioxidant, silychristin found in milk thistle helps with jaundice by protecting liver cells from damage and inflammation.
Silydianin: A flavonolignan, silydianin has been shown to have hepatoprotective properties, making it beneficial in the management of jaundice.
Isosilybin: This bioactive compound found in milk thistle helps with jaundice by exhibiting antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and liver-protecting effects.
remedy_recipe: In a cup, combine 1 teaspoon of crushed milk thistle seeds with 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the mixture for 10-15 minutes to allow the active compounds to infuse.
Strain the tea to remove the seeds, resulting in a milk thistle extract.
Consume the tea, up to three times a day, preferably between meals.
Continue this regimen for several weeks, unless directed otherwise by a healthcare professional.

remedies.remedy_desc: Dandelion tea helps with jaundice because it promotes the production of bile in the liver, which aids in breaking down bilirubin, the compound that accumulates in the blood during jaundice, causing the skin and eyes to turn yellow. The tea is rich in antioxidants, which support liver function and help repair damaged liver cells, reducing the severity of jaundice. Dandelion tea also has diuretic properties, which increase urine production, facilitating the removal of toxins and excess bilirubin from the body. Furthermore, it contains taraxacin, a compound known for its ability to protect the liver from damage and promote bile flow. Lastly, the tea's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce inflammation in the liver, promoting healing and alleviating jaundice symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of dandelions are used in medicinal tea for jaundice due to their ability to promote bile production and flow, which aids in the elimination of bilirubin.
Leaves: Rich in antioxidants, dandelion leaves are included in jaundice tea to support liver function and help in the body's detoxification process.
Flowers: Dandelion flowers are used in tea for jaundice patients because of their anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the liver and promote healing.
remedy_constituents: Taraxacin: This component of dandelion stimulates the production of bile, helping to eliminate waste products from the body and reduce the buildup of bilirubin that causes jaundice.
Taraxacerin: This diuretic constituent helps increase urine production, which aids in the elimination of waste products, including bilirubin, from the body and may improve symptoms of jaundice.
Inulin: This soluble fiber promotes the growth of beneficial gut bacteria, which can help improve digestion and absorption of nutrients, thus aiding in the management of jaundice.

Sesquiterpene lactones: These compounds have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce liver inflammation and oxidative stress, thereby alleviating jaundice symptoms.
Phenolic acids: These compounds have been found to have hepatoprotective effects, which means they can protect the liver from damage and promote its healing, thus helping to manage jaundice.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2-3 handfuls of fresh dandelion flowers, ensuring they are free from pesticides or pollutants, and rinse them gently under running water.
Submerge the flowers in 4 cups of boiling water, then reduce heat and let it simmer for 10-15 minutes to allow the active compounds to extract.
Strain the liquid to remove flower particles, and let it cool down to a comfortable drinking temperature.
Squeeze the juice of half a lemon into the dandelion tea for added vitamin C and improved taste.
Consume 3-4 cups of this tea daily, preferably before meals, for several weeks to help manage jaundice symptoms. Always consult a healthcare professional before starting any new herbal treatment.

remedies.remedy_desc: Turmeric tea helps with jaundice because it contains curcumin, a powerful antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compound. This compound stimulates the production of bile, which aids in the breakdown of bilirubin, the yellow pigment responsible for jaundice. Furthermore, curcumin has hepatoprotective properties that protect the liver from damage and support its functioning, which is crucial in managing jaundice. Additionally, turmeric tea's anti-inflammatory effects can help reduce inflammation in the liver, further supporting its ability to process bilirubin effectively. However, it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using turmeric tea as a complementary treatment for jaundice, as it may interact with certain medications and underlying health conditions.
remedy_parts: Rhizomes: Turmeric's underground stem, or rhizome, contains potent curcuminoids, which have been studied for their potential to improve liver function and reduce jaundice symptoms.
Leaves: Rich in antioxidants, turmeric leaves have been used in traditional medicine to support liver health and help manage jaundice by promoting bile production and flow.
Flowers: Turmeric flowers contain bioactive compounds that may help protect the liver, stimulate bile secretion, and alleviate jaundice-related symptoms.
Seeds: Although less commonly used, turmeric seeds possess medicinal properties that can aid in detoxification and support liver function, which might benefit jaundice patients.
remedy_constituents: Curcumin: This is the most active constituent of turmeric, and it has been shown to have anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and liver-protecting properties, making it helpful in treating jaundice.

Demethoxycurcumin: This compound also has anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and liver-protecting effects, and it can help to reduce bilirubin levels in the body, which is beneficial in treating jaundice.
Bisdemethoxycurcumin: This compound has similar properties as curcumin and demethoxycurcumin, and it can help to treat jaundice by reducing oxidative stress and protecting liver cells from damage.

Curcuminoids: This term refers to a group of compounds that includes curcumin, demethoxycurcumin, and bisdemethoxycurcumin. Curcuminoids are known to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to reduce bilirubin levels and treat jaundice.

Essential oils: Turmeric contains a variety of essential oils that have liver-protecting and antioxidant properties. These oils can help to reduce oxidative stress, protect liver cells, and lower bilirubin levels in the body, all of which can be beneficial in treating jaundice.
remedy_recipe: In a saucepan, combine 1 cup of water, 1/2 teaspoon of turmeric powder, and 1/4 teaspoon of black pepper (which enhances turmeric's absorption); bring to a boil.
Reduce heat, let it simmer for 10 minutes to allow the beneficial compounds to infuse.
Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the turmeric residue.
Add honey or lemon to taste (optional), but avoid adding milk as it can interfere with turmeric's absorption.
Wait for the tea to cool down to a drinkable temperature before consumption, ideally 2-3 times a day for relief from jaundice symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Chicory tea helps with jaundice because it contains high levels of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce the damage caused by free radicals and inflammation in the liver, the organ responsible for bilirubin processing. The tea's bitter compounds can stimulate the production of bile and digestive juices, aiding in the elimination of bilirubin from the body. Additionally, chicory tea has been shown to protect and support liver function, which is crucial for those suffering from jaundice. Its diuretic properties can also help flush toxins out of the body, further supporting the liver and promoting overall health. Lastly, chicory tea is a caffeine-free alternative to other beverages, making it a suitable option for individuals looking to avoid stimulants during their recovery from jaundice.
remedy_parts: Roots: Chicory roots are commonly used in medicinal teas for jaundice due to their ability to promote bile production and flow.
Leaves: The leaves of the chicory plant are used in tea to treat jaundice as they have hepato-protective properties that help repair liver damage.
Flowers: Chicory flowers are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, making them beneficial for treating jaundice when used in tea.
Buds: The buds of the chicory plant are known to stimulate the production of bile, which aids in the treatment of jaundice.
Seeds: Chicory seeds are often used in teas for jaundice due to their ability to detoxify the liver and promote the excretion of excess bilirubin.
remedy_constituents: Inulin: Stimulates bile production, helping to eliminate bilirubin from the body.
Sesquiterpene Lactones: Promote bile flow, aiding in the elimination of waste products and reducing jaundice symptoms.
Taraxasterol: Supports liver function, helping to improve the processing and elimination of bilirubin.
Cichoric Acid: Acts as an antioxidant, protecting the liver from damage caused by toxins and free radicals.
Flavonoids: Possess anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation and swelling in the liver, a common symptom of jaundice.
remedy_recipe: Rinse 2 tablespoons of dried chicory roots thoroughly, then place them in a pot and add 4 cups of water.
Bring the water to a boil, then reduce heat and let the mixture simmer, covered, for 15-20 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the chicory roots, then let it cool down to a drinkable temperature.
Drink 2-3 cups of the chicory tea daily, preferably between meals, for several days to help with jaundice.

remedies.remedy_desc: Yarrow tea helps with jaundice because it promotes liver function and bile production, which are crucial in addressing the root cause of jaundice, as the condition is often associated with impaired liver functions and reduced bile secretion. The tea's active compounds, like flavonoids and alkaloids, have been shown to protect liver cells, reduce inflammation, and aid in tissue regeneration, all of which can contribute to improved liver health and overall reduction of jaundice symptoms. Furthermore, yarrow tea's diuretic properties encourage the elimination of waste and toxins from the body, which may help speed up the recovery process and alleviate jaundice-related discomforts. Lastly, yarrow tea has been used traditionally for its blood purifying effects, which may help reduce bilirubin levels, one of the key factors contributing to jaundice's characteristic yellowing of the skin and eyes. However, it's important to consult a healthcare professional before incorporating yarrow tea or any other alternative remedy into a jaundice treatment plan.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers of the yarrow plant contain flavonoids, which have been shown to help improve liver function, making them useful in treating jaundice.
Leaves: The leaves of the yarrow plant are rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, which can help reduce inflammation in the liver and improve bile flow.
Stems: The stems of the yarrow plant contain lactones and tannins, which have been shown to have a positive effect on liver function and can help in the treatment of jaundice.

Roots: The roots of the yarrow plant have been used traditionally to treat liver ailments, and contain alkaloids, which have been shown to have a positive effect on liver function and bile production.
remedy_constituents: Sesquiterpene lactones: These compounds exhibit anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which can help alleviate liver inflammation and bile duct spasms associated with jaundice.
Flavonoids: Flavonoids like luteolin and apigenin have hepatoprotective effects, helping to protect the liver from damage and promoting its recovery in jaundice.
Alkaloids: Alkaloids in yarrow, such as protopine and berberine, possess cholagogue properties, stimulating the flow of bile from the liver and gallbladder, aiding in jaundice treatment.

Volatile oils: The volatile oils in yarrow, including α- and β-pinene, limonene, and sabinene, have been shown to promote liver function and detoxification processes, beneficial for managing jaundice.
Coumarins: Coumarins like umbelliferone and scopoletin exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities, helping to reduce liver stress and oxidative damage in jaundice conditions.
remedy_recipe: Harvest or acquire 2-3 tablespoons of fresh yarrow leaves and flowers, ensuring they are free from pesticides and pollutants.
Rinse the yarrow leaves and flowers gently under cold running water, then let them air dry or pat them gently with a clean cloth.
Place the dried yarrow herbs in a teapot and pour 16 ounces of boiling water over them, allowing the tea to steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the yarrow tea to remove the plant material, then let it cool down to a comfortable drinking temperature.
Drink 3-4 cups of yarrow tea daily, between meals, for a period of 7-10 days to help treat jaundice symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Greater celandine tea helps with jaundice because it contains alkaloids, such as chelidonine, that can stimulate the production of bile and support liver function. These alkaloids can also help to protect the liver from damage, which can be beneficial in treating jaundice. Additionally, greater celandine has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce inflammation in the liver and bile ducts, which can contribute to the development of jaundice. Furthermore, greater celandine tea can help to improve digestion and support the overall health of the liver and gallbladder, which can help to prevent the buildup of bilirubin that causes jaundice. However, it is important to note that greater celandine tea should not be used as a sole treatment for jaundice and should be used in conjunction with medical treatment prescribed by a healthcare professional.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the greater celandine plant are used in making medicinal tea for jaundice due to their high anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties which help to support liver function.
Flowers: The flowers of the greater celandine plant are known for their ability to stimulate bile secretion which aids in the treatment of jaundice.
Stems: The stems of the greater celandine plant contain alkaloids that have a positive effect on the liver and are used in the preparation of medicinal tea for jaundice.
Roots: The roots of the greater celandine plant are rich in medicinal compounds, including alkaloids, that help to treat liver ailments like jaundice.
remedy_constituents: Berberine: This alkaloid helps with jaundice by exerting a choleretic effect, which promotes the flow of bile from the liver and speeds up the elimination of bilirubin.
Chelidonine: This alkaloid is known for its cholagogue properties, which help increase bile production and support liver function, thereby aiding in the treatment of jaundice.
Sanguinarine: This alkaloid has anti-inflammatory and anti-infective properties that can help reduce liver inflammation and combat potential infections associated with jaundice.
Chelerythrine: This alkaloid exhibits hepatoprotective effects, which means it can protect the liver from damage and promote healing, making it beneficial for jaundice treatment.
remedy_recipe: Harvest: Pick 2-3 leaves and a small stem from a mature greater celandine plant, ensuring it's free from pesticides and environmental pollutants.
Clean: Gently rinse the harvested leaves and stem under cool running water, then pat dry with a clean towel.
Steep: Place the clean plant material in a cup, pour 8 ounces of boiling water over it, cover, and let steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain: Remove the leaves and stem, and discard them; retain the dark yellow liquid (tea).
Dose: Drink 3 cups of this tea daily, between meals, for 2-3 weeks to help treat jaundice. Consult a healthcare professional before starting any herbal treatment.

remedies.remedy_desc: Artichoke tea helps with jaundice because it stimulates the production of bile, which aids in the digestion of fats and the removal of waste products from the body. The antioxidants present in artichoke, such as cynarin and silymarin, protect the liver from damage and promote its regeneration. These antioxidants also help to reduce inflammation and improve liver function, which can help to reduce the symptoms of jaundice. Additionally, artichoke tea has diuretic properties, which can help to flush toxins from the body and support the overall health of the liver and gallbladder. Furthermore, artichoke tea has been shown to increase the flow of bile from the liver to the intestines, which can help to break down and eliminate the bilirubin that causes jaundice.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Artichoke leaves are often used in medicinal teas for jaundice due to their reported ability to promote healthy liver function and bile production.
Buds: The buds of the artichoke plant are utilized in teas for jaundice because of their high antioxidant content, which may help protect the liver from damage.
Seeds: Artichoke seeds are included in teas for jaundice thanks to their concentration of cynarin, a compound believed to aid in bile production and liver health.
Stems: The stems of the artichoke plant are sometimes used in medicinal teas for jaundice as they contain inulin, a type of fiber that may support overall gut and liver health.
remedy_constituents: Silymarin: A powerful antioxidant that helps protect liver cells and promotes their regeneration, making it helpful in treating jaundice caused by liver damage.
Cynarin: Boosts bile production, aiding in the digestion of fats and elimination of wastes, which can help reduce the symptoms of jaundice.
Flavonoids: Possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that support liver function and overall health, beneficial in managing jaundice.
Chlorogenic acid: Another antioxidant that helps protect liver cells and tissues, contributing to the improvement of jaundice symptoms.
Beta-carotene: A provitamin A carotenoid that supports liver health and promotes healing, reducing the severity of jaundice.
Magnesium: Contributes to enzyme activation, maintaining bile production, and overall liver function, supporting the treatment of jaundice.
Potassium: Supports electrolyte balance, essential in liver function and the excretion of bilirubin, aiding in the management of jaundice.
remedy_recipe: Boil 2 cups of water in a pot for 5 minutes, then add 1 tablespoon of dried artichoke leaves.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for 10 minutes to allow the nutrients to infuse.
Strain the tea to remove artichoke leaves, leaving only the liquid.
Allow the artichoke tea to cool down to a drinkable temperature.
Sip the artichoke tea slowly, 2-3 times a day, after meals to aid in jaundice treatment.

remedies.remedy_desc: Birch tea helps with jaundice because it contains substances that promote liver health and detoxification, such as betulinic acid and other flavonoids. These compounds support the liver's ability to process bilirubin, a yellow pigment responsible for jaundice, more efficiently. Additionally, birch tea acts as a diuretic, increasing urine production and helping the body eliminate toxins and excess bilirubin. The anti-inflammatory properties of birch tea also reduce inflammation in the liver, enhancing its function and further aiding in the recovery from jaundice. Furthermore, birch tea has antioxidant effects, protecting liver cells from damage caused by free radicals and supporting overall liver health.
remedy_parts: Bark: The bark of the birch tree is commonly used in making medicinal tea for jaundice due to its ability to help the body eliminate toxins.
Leaves: Birch leaves are used in tea for jaundice because of their high tannin content, which can help reduce inflammation and promote healing.
Buds: Birch buds are a rich source of antioxidants and are used in tea to support liver function, which can help alleviate jaundice symptoms.
Roots: The roots of the birch tree contain betulinic acid, which has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, making it useful in tea for jaundice.
Stems: Birch stems are used in tea for jaundice due to their diuretic properties, which can help eliminate excess bile from the body.
remedy_constituents: Betulin: Betulin has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and hepatoprotective properties, which can help reduce liver inflammation and promote healing in jaundice.
Betulinic Acid: Betulinic acid has been found to have anti-fibrotic effects, which can help prevent the progression of liver damage and fibrosis in jaundice.
Lupeol: Lupeol has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help protect liver cells from damage and promote recovery in jaundice.

Pentacyclic Triterpenes: Pentacyclic triterpenes in birch, such as oleanolic and ursolic acids, have been found to have hepatoprotective effects, which can help protect the liver and promote recovery in jaundice.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids in birch, such as quercetin and hyperoside, have been found to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help protect liver cells and promote recovery in jaundice.
remedy_recipe: Harvest young birch leaves, ensuring they are free from pollution, then rinse and drain them, setting aside 2 cups worth.
Place the leaves in a pot, covering them with 4 cups of distilled water, and slowly bring the mixture to a simmer over medium-low heat.
Allow the tea to steep for 15-20 minutes, ensuring the water temperature remains below boiling point, then remove from heat.
Strain the tea, discarding the leaves, and let it cool down to a comfortable drinking temperature.
Drink 1 cup of the birch tea, three times a day, before meals, for best results in managing jaundice symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Nettle tea helps with jaundice because it contains naturally occurring nutrients and compounds that can potentially support liver function, including chlorophyll, flavonoids, and antioxidants. These constituents may help to protect liver cells from damage and promote healing, thus aiding in the body's natural process of eliminating bilirubin, a key factor in jaundice. Furthermore, nettle tea has diuretic properties, which can help the body flush out toxins and excess bilirubin through urination. Additionally, the tea's anti-inflammatory effects may alleviate stress on the liver, allowing it to better regulate bilirubin levels. However, it is important to note that while nettle tea can be a beneficial supplement to jaundice treatment, it should not replace medical advice or prescribed medications; individuals should consult with a healthcare professional before incorporating nettle tea or any other alternative therapies into their jaundice management plan.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Nettle leaves are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help support liver function and alleviate jaundice symptoms.
Stems: Nettle stems contain high levels of chlorophyll, which can help detoxify the body and support the liver in breaking down bilirubin, thereby reducing jaundice.
Roots: Nettle roots have diuretic properties, which can help eliminate excess fluids and toxins from the body, supporting liver function and reducing jaundice.
Flowers: Nettle flowers contain flavonoids, which have antioxidant properties and can help protect liver cells from damage, reducing the symptoms of jaundice.
remedy_constituents: Chlorophyll: Nettle leaves are rich in chlorophyll, which has been found to have anti-oxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. These properties can help reduce the inflammation and oxidative stress associated with jaundice.

Carotenoids: Nettle contains carotenoids, particularly beta-carotene, which can help improve liver function. A well-functioning liver is essential in treating jaundice, a condition characterized by the accumulation of bilirubin in the body due to liver problems.

Flavonoids: Nettle is rich in flavonoids, such as rutin and quercetin, which have been shown to protect and support liver health. This can help reduce bilirubin levels, a key factor in managing jaundice.
Minerals: Nettle contains essential minerals like iron, magnesium, and calcium. These minerals can help support liver function and overall health, contributing to the management of jaundice.
Vitamins: Nettle is a great source of vitamins A, C, and K, which are all important for liver health and overall well-being. A healthy liver is crucial for managing jaundice.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2-3 handfuls of fresh nettle leaves, ensuring they are free of pesticides and other contaminants, and rinse them thoroughly in cold water.
Crush or chop the nettle leaves to expose their surfaces and allow for maximum release of beneficial compounds during the steeping process, then place them in a teapot or heat-resistant container.
Boil 4 cups (1 liter) of water, then pour it over the nettle leaves in the teapot.
Allow the tea to steep for 15-20 minutes to extract the medicinal components from the nettle leaves; the longer the steeping time, the stronger the tea's flavor and potency.

Strain the tea to remove the leaves, then let it cool to a drinkable temperature before consuming 2-3 cups per day, preferably between meals, for a period of 7-10 days or as directed by a healthcare professional.

remedies.remedy_desc: Chamomile tea helps with jaundice because it contains antioxidants that can help reduce inflammation and damage to the liver, which is the organ responsible for processing bilirubin, the substance that causes jaundice. The liver-protecting properties of chamomile tea can help improve liver function, allowing it to process bilirubin more efficiently and reduce the severity of jaundice symptoms. Chamomile tea also has mild diuretic effects, which can help the body eliminate excess bilirubin through urine. Additionally, chamomile tea has anti-spasmodic properties that can help alleviate abdominal discomfort and pain associated with jaundice. However, it is important to note that chamomile tea should not be used as a substitute for medical treatment for jaundice, and anyone experiencing symptoms of jaundice should consult a healthcare professional.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Chamomile flowers are commonly used in medicinal tea for jaundice due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
Leaves: The leaves of chamomile contain therapeutic compounds that help support liver function, making them a suitable option for jaundice treatment.
Seeds: Chamomile seeds are rich in essential oils, which can help stimulate bile production and promote healthy digestion, aiding in jaundice recovery.
remedy_constituents: Apigenin: This flavonoid in chamomile has been found to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to protect the liver and promote its function, thus beneficial in managing jaundice.
Luteolin: With its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, luteolin helps reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the liver, aiding in the treatment of jaundice.

α-Bisabolol: This terpene alcohol found in chamomile exhibits anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and wound-healing properties, contributing to the reduction of inflammation and healing of liver damage caused by jaundice.
Chamazulene: This aromatic compound in chamomile demonstrates antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, protecting liver cells from oxidative stress and thereby helping in jaundice management.
Matricin: A precursor to chamazulene, matricin possesses anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which can help alleviate liver inflammation and pain associated with jaundice.
remedy_recipe: Boil one cup of water in a pot for 3-5 minutes.
Add one teaspoon of dried chamomile flowers to the boiling water.
Reduce heat to low, cover the pot, and let it steep for 5-10 minutes.

Drink the tea, up to three times a day, to help treat jaundice symptoms. (Note: While chamomile tea is generally safe, people with allergies to ragweed or other plants in the daisy family should avoid drinking it. If your symptoms persist, consult with a healthcare professional.)

remedies.remedy_desc: Burdock tea helps with jaundice because it promotes liver function and detoxification. The root of the burdock plant contains active compounds that support the liver's natural cleansing processes, which can be beneficial in reducing jaundice symptoms. These compounds include sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and volatile oils, which have been shown to protect and stimulate liver cells. Additionally, burdock tea exhibits diuretic properties, helping the body eliminate excess bilirubin through urine and, consequently, decreasing the yellowing of the skin and eyes associated with jaundice. The tea's anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects also contribute to overall liver health, supporting the body's ability to manage and recover from jaundice. It is essential to consult a healthcare professional for proper diagnosis and treatment of jaundice, as burdock tea can be used as a complementary therapy alongside medical interventions.

Red clover tea helps with jaundice because it contains high levels of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that support liver function and detoxification. The tea's active ingredients, such as isoflavones and genistein, have been shown to protect the liver from damage and promote the production of bile, which is essential for breaking down bilirubin, a substance that accumulates in the blood during jaundice. Furthermore, red clover tea may help stimulate the flow of bile from the liver to the gallbladder, aiding in the elimination of waste products and toxins that can contribute to jaundice. Additionally, red clover tea has diuretic properties that can help flush out excess fluids and waste from the body, reducing the risk of liver congestion and further supporting liver health. Lastly, red clover tea is also known to have a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help reduce stress and promote overall well-being, contributing to the body's ability to heal and recover from jaundice.

Fennel tea helps with jaundice because it has potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that aid in liver function and detoxification. The tea's active components, such as anethole, fenchone, and estragole, stimulate the production of bile, which is essential for breaking down fats and removing waste products from the body. This increased bile production helps to eliminate bilirubin, a primary cause of jaundice, from the body more efficiently. Furthermore, fennel tea's mild diuretic effect promotes urination, which also assists in removing excess bilirubin. Additionally, fennel tea's hepatoprotective properties shield the liver from damage and support overall liver health, contributing to the alleviation of jaundice symptoms.

Licorice tea helps with jaundice because it stimulates the production of bile, which aids in the breakdown of bilirubin, a substance that accumulates in the body during jaundice, leading to its characteristic yellowing of the skin and eyes. The liquorice root, from which the tea is derived, contains flavonoids that promote liver health and help in detoxification, thereby reducing the workload on the liver and accelerating the recovery process. Additionally, licorice tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate any discomfort or pain associated with jaundice. However, it is important to note that while licorice tea can be a beneficial adjunct therapy, it should not replace conventional medical treatment, and individuals with jaundice should consult with a healthcare professional before starting any new supplements or teas. Lastly, it is essential to consume licorice tea in moderation, as excessive consumption can lead to side effects such as high blood pressure and potassium depletion.

Manjistha tea helps with jaundice because it contains antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that reduce liver inflammation and promote bile production. The herb's active compounds, such as anthraquinones and xanthones, stimulate the liver's detoxification process and support healthy liver function. Manjistha tea also improves the flow of bile from the liver to the gallbladder, aiding in digestion and reducing the risk of gallstones. Furthermore, it helps eliminate toxins from the body, reducing the burden on the liver and supporting overall liver health. Lastly, manjistha tea has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat jaundice and other liver-related conditions, demonstrating its potential therapeutic benefits.

Ginger tea helps with jaundice because it contains powerful antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that support liver function, which is essential for recovering from jaundice. The active ingredient in ginger, gingerol, stimulates the production of bile in the liver, aiding in the breakdown of bilirubin, a key factor in managing jaundice. Additionally, ginger tea supports digestion and eases stomach discomfort, addressing symptoms associated with jaundice. Furthermore, its diuretic properties promote urination, helping the body eliminate excess bilirubin. Ginger tea's ability to strengthen the immune system also assists in combating infections, common in individuals with jaundice caused by hepatitis or other liver ailments.

Peppermint tea helps with jaundice because it promotes the production of bile in the liver, aiding in the elimination of bilirubin from the body. The antioxidants present in peppermint tea, such as rosmarinic acid, help protect liver cells from damage and support liver function. Additionally, peppermint tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the liver, which is often a contributing factor in jaundice. The soothing and calming effects of peppermint tea can also help alleviate digestive issues associated with jaundice, such as nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain. Lastly, peppermint tea is a natural and caffeine-free alternative to other beverages, making it a safe and gentle option for individuals seeking to support their liver health and manage jaundice symptoms.

Coriander tea helps with jaundice because it contains high levels of antioxidants and essential oils, such as cineole, borneol, limonene, alpha-pinene, and decylaldehyde, which aid in liver function and detoxification.
